title: reseda phyteuma subsp. phyteuma
intro: Reseda phyteuma subsp. phyteuma, also known as the common reedma or reedmace, is a plant species in the family Boraginaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. It has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues. The herbal preparations of Reseda phyteuma subsp. phyteuma include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat colds, coughs, and skin conditions.
